name: Polystyrene Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Polystyrene. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for Polystyrene surface cleaning without thermal damage

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength providing good absorption in Polystyrene with minimal thermal penetration

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for precise cleaning control on Polystyrene surfaces

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for efficient cleaning with thermal management

energyDensity.description: Fluence threshold for effective contaminant removal without substrate damage

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for controlled ablation of Polystyrene surface contaminants

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform cleaning coverage

passCount.description: Recommended number of passes for complete contaminant removal

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap for uniform cleaning pattern

power.description: Typical range for polymer cleaning to avoid thermal damage; starting at 50W for light contamination on PS, max limited by PS melting point (~240°C) and low thermal conductivity. Source: Studies on Nd:YAG laser cleaning of plastics (e.g., J. Appl. Phys. 2018).

scan_speed.description: Effective for contamination removal without substrate ablation; slower (500-1000 mm/s) for heavy contamination, faster for light; optimal balances fluence and coverage. Source: Industry manuals from IPG Photonics and laser cleaning process optimization papers.

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for cost-effective cleaning of PS; shorter pulses (ps/fs) minimize HAZ but ns sufficient for contamination removal without deep polymer damage. Source: Laser ablation studies on polymers (Opt. Lasers Eng. 2020).

repetition_rate.description: Balances cleaning speed and heat accumulation in low-conductivity PS; higher rates increase throughput but risk melting. Source: Academic literature on pulsed laser cleaning (Appl. Surf. Sci. 2019).

energy_density.description: Below PS ablation threshold (~2-4 J/cm² at 1064 nm) for safe cleaning; typical for removing contaminants while preserving substrate. Source: Fluence threshold measurements for polystyrene (J. Phys. D: Appl. Phys. 2017).

spot_size.description: Smaller spots for precise surface prep on PS, larger for broad areas; 200 μm balances resolution and efficiency. Source: Equipment specs from Coherent and Trumpf laser systems.

pass_count.description: Multi-pass for thorough removal of heavy contamination; single pass for light; recommended 2 for uniform PS cleaning without overexposure. Source: Case studies in industrial laser cleaning (Proc. SPIE 2021).

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage on irregular PS surfaces; 50% optimal for efficiency and minimal heat buildup. Source: Process optimization in laser scanning (Opt. Express 2016).
